title: lagenocarpus minarum var. paucifolius
intro: Lagenocarpus minarum var. paucifolius, also known as the few-leaved bag flower, is a plant species belonging to the family Thymelaeaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial activities. The plant's health benefits include its potential to reduce fever, soothe respiratory issues, and aid in wound healing. Herbal preparations of Lagenocarpus minarum var. paucifolius are typically made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water to create a tea that can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
